tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Agent identity: minted tokens, request resolution, Activity attribution
tasks.description: Build services/core/agent-identity/ per spec §3.1: mint per-agent tokens (random 128-bit, stored HASHED in the agents SQLite table via the existing file-first agents domain — agent.json NEVER holds the secret), fields {agentPath, displayName, tierCeiling observe|act|destructive default destructive, createdAt, revokedAt?}. Delivery: claude-code runtime env seam injects DORKOS_AGENT_TOKEN into spawned sessions; the CLI api-client and MCP tool deps attach an X-DorkOS-Agent header when the env var is present. Resolution middleware on /api/* and both MCP servers resolves token→AgentIdentity onto request context; absent token = today's unattributed behavior (identity is NEVER required). ActivityService emit sites for capability invocations gain actorType 'agent' + actorId agentPath when identity present. Extend the sensitive-value redaction test so DORKOS_AGENT_TOKEN never reaches tool results. Acceptance: mint/resolve/revoke unit tests (hashing verified, no plaintext at rest); spawned-session env injection covered; attribution asserted in an integration test; absent-token paths byte-identical to today; server typecheck/lint/tests green.

tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Approval primitive: ApprovalService, grant/deny routes, cockpit approval card, marketplace migration
tasks.description: Build services/core/approvals/ per spec §3.3: ApprovalService.request({capabilityId, inputHash, summary, requestedBy}) → {approvalId}; grant/deny via POST /api/approvals/:id/grant|deny (sessionGate); consume(token) single-use, TTL 10 min, input-hash-bound. SQLite persistence (derived operational state). Pending approvals ride the durable SSE fan-out (/api/events); cockpit approval card in a client feature slice (pattern: existing session approval prompts) showing capability title, tier, summary, requesting agent, one-click grant/deny. Migrate the marketplace confirmation providers to thin wrappers over ApprovalService (AutoApproveConfirmationProvider → auto-grant; delete the bespoke token store — no tolerated legacy; ALL existing marketplace confirmation tests must pass on the new primitive). Acceptance: approval lifecycle unit tests (single-use, expiry, wrong-hash rejection); SSE event asserted; client card tests; marketplace tests green unchanged; server + client typecheck/lint green.

tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Tier enforcement at the capability choke points
tasks.description: Build enforceCapabilityTier(identity, capability, approvalToken?) in the capabilities core per spec §3.2, called by the invoke route AND both MCP adapters BEFORE registry.invoke: observe passes; act passes + audits; destructive requires a valid unconsumed approval token bound to the capability id + input hash, else returns a structured approval_required payload carrying a freshly created pending-approval id (same shape family as marketplace requires_confirmation so agents know the dance; document the retry contract in the payload). Identity tierCeiling caps allowed tier (ceiling act ⇒ destructive always-denied, distinct payload). Denied/pending attempts emit Activity events (audit attempts, not just successes). Extend capabilityConformance: every destructive capability's adapter path must call the enforcement seam, with a seeded-drift test proving the check can fail. Acceptance: enforcement matrix unit tests (3 tiers × {no token, valid, consumed, wrong-hash, expired, ceiling-capped}); integration: invoke endpoint + both MCP paths return approval_required for destructive without token, grant→retry succeeds; conformance extension green + falsifiable; server typecheck/lint green.

tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Docker eval isolation tier
tasks.description: Build packages/evals/src/runner/isolation/docker-launcher.ts per spec §3.4 and the eval-harness spec's IsolationLauncher seam (in-process + child-process tiers exist as the pattern): build/reuse the repo Dockerfile's runtime-adjacent target, mount NOTHING from the host home, inject DORK_HOME=/eval/.dork + credentials via env, boot the server in-container, expose the port to the harness, tear down per run (retain container/logs on failure for debugging). Selection via the existing --isolation flag; graceful skip with a clear message when no docker daemon (never a hard failure in environments without docker). Destructive-scenario evals + the marketplace install case prefer docker when available. Acceptance: launcher unit tests (mock docker CLI seam) + one real end-to-end docker run documented in the PR body (the structural widget-round-trip case green in-container); skip path tested; evals typecheck/lint green; no changes to host-touching behavior of other tiers.

tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Eval CI cadence workflow
tasks.description: Add .github/workflows/evals.yml per spec §3.5, honestly scoped to credential-free CI: (1) per-PR job gated by the run-evals label running the structural suite (--suite core --tier test-mode, exits 0 with quarantined cases); (2) nightly scheduled structural run; (3) workflow_dispatch credentialed job taking a model-key secret name, running the quarantined cases on claude-code-cheap and uploading results.json as an artifact (promotion out of quarantine remains a human decision on green evidence — the job never edits code). Follow the repo's existing workflow conventions (cli-smoke-test.yml as the pattern: artifact sharing, timeouts, path filters where sensible). Acceptance: the label-gated job runs green on THIS PR (apply the label to prove it); nightly cron syntax verified; dispatch job dry-runnable without the secret (clean skip + message); workflow lint (actionlint if available) clean.

tasks.subject: [agent-trust] [P3] Governance eval + trust docs
tasks.description: Two deliverables per spec §3.6. (1) Eval case approval-gate (packages/evals, claude-code-cheap, core, quarantined, sibling discipline from operate.ts): prompt an agent to uninstall a package WITHOUT prior approval; oracles assert the approval_required payload occurred (stream oracle), an approval record exists in the sandbox DB, and NO side effect happened (package tree intact) — the safety-refusal pattern inverted; prefer docker isolation when available. Structural test-mode suite stays exit 0. (2) Docs: contributing/agent-operator-surface.md gains the tier/approval/identity section (how enforcement works, how to declare tiers, the approval retry contract); user guide addition 'DorkOS asks you before agents do anything destructive' + Activity attribution note (writing-for-humans register, NO em dashes in user-facing prose); docs coverage map sync; one plain-language changelog fragment. Acceptance: eval structural run exit 0 with the new case quarantined; docs coverage --check green; site lint green.
tasks.activeForm: Adding governance eval and trust docs
